title: My Mate Bill
content:
آ آ آ آ Thatï؟½s his saddle on the tie-beam,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And themï؟½s his spurs up there
آ آ آ آ On the wall-plate over yonderï؟½
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ You ken see they ainï؟½t a pair.

آ آ آ آ For the daddy of all the stockmen
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ As ever come mustering here
آ آ آ آ Was killed in the flaming mulga,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ A-yarding a bald-faced steer.

آ آ آ آ They say as heï؟½s gone to heaven,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And shook off all worldly cares
آ آ آ آ But I canï؟½t sight Bill in a halo
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Set up on three blinded hairs.

آ آ آ آ In heaven! what next I wonder,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ For strike me pink and blue,
آ آ آ آ If I see whatever in thunder
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Theyï؟½ll find for Bill to do.

آ آ آ آ Heï؟½d never make one of them angels,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ With faces as white as chalk,
آ آ آ آ All wool to the toes like hoggets,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And wings like an eagle-hawk.

آ آ آ آ He couldnï؟½t ï؟½arp for apples,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ His voice had tones as jarred,
آ آ آ آ And heï؟½d no more ear than a bald-faced steer,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Or calves in a branding yard.

آ آ آ آ He could sit on a bucking brumbie
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Like a nob in an easy chair,
آ آ آ آ And chop his name with a greenhide fall
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ On the flank of a flying steer.

آ آ آ آ He could show them saints in glory
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ The way that a fall should drop,
آ آ آ آ But sit on a throneï؟½not William,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Unless they could make it prop.

آ آ آ آ He mightnï؟½t freeze to the seraphs,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Or chum with the cherubim,
آ آ آ آ But if ever them seraph johnnies
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Get a-poking it like at himï؟½

آ آ آ آ Well! if thereï؟½s hide in heaven,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And silk for to make a lash,
آ آ آ آ Heï؟½ll yard ï؟½em all in the Jasper Lake
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ In a blinded lightning flash.

آ آ آ آ If the heavenly hosts get boxed now,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ As mobs most always will,
آ آ آ آ Whoï؟½ll cut ï؟½em out like William,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Or draft on a camp like Bill?

آ آ آ آ An ï؟½orseman would find it awkward
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ At first with a push that flew,
آ آ آ آ But blame my cats if I know what else
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Theyï؟½ll find for Bill to do.

آ آ آ آ Itï؟½s hard if there ainï؟½t no cattle,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And perhaps theyï؟½ll let him sleep,
آ آ آ آ And wake him up at the judgment
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ To draft those goats and sheep.

آ آ آ آ Itï؟½s playing it low on William,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ But perhaps heï؟½ll buckle to,
آ آ آ آ To show them high-toned seraphs
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ What a Mulga man can do.

آ آ آ آ If they saddles a big-boned angel,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ With a turn of speed, of course,
آ آ آ آ As can spiel like a four-year brumbie,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And prop like an old camp horse,

آ آ آ آ And puts Bill up with a snaffle,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ A four or five inch spur,
آ آ آ آ And eighteen foot of greenhide
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ To chop the blinded furï؟½

آ آ آ آ Heï؟½ll yard them blamed Angoras
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ In a way that itï؟½s safe to swear
آ آ آ آ Will make them tony seraphs
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Sit back on their thrones and stare.
meaning:
Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton)'s contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "My Mate Bill"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
